international.region: Thailand · Sea level (Bangkok/Phuket) to 2,565 m (Doi Inthanon north)
international.pitch: From golden wats and floating markets to limestone-karst islands and Michelin-street-food — Thailand delivers five-star scenery at three-star prices, and Indians land visa-free.

climate.note: Peak season: dry, cool nights, zero rain, best beach and city conditions
Still dry and clear; slightly warmer but eminently comfortable
Dry season end; heat building but manageable; good beach visibility
Hottest month (36-38°C); Songkran (13-15 Apr) is chaotic and fun; hotel prices spike
Southwest monsoon onset; rough seas cancel island tours; afternoon downpours daily
Heavy monsoon; Phuket/Krabi west coast beaches closed; flooding possible in Bangkok
Peak monsoon rain; intermittent but severe storms; budget travellers only
Sustained heavy rains; Chiang Mai and Bangkok both wet; sea dangerously rough
Monsoon peak; lowest tourist traffic; some east coast islands briefly accessible
Monsoon tapering in Bangkok/Phuket; Koh Samui/east coast still wet; good deals
Southwest monsoon over; west-coast beaches clear; avoid Koh Samui (northeast monsoon)
Cool dry peak; best all-round conditions; festive crowds and Christmas pricing

international.whyGo: Thailand is the gold standard Southeast Asia first-timer destination — absurdly photogenic temples, crystalline Andaman waters, and a street-food culture that punches above Michelin. It handles first-time international Indian travellers with ease: Hindi menus in tourist zones, zero visa hassle, and a price point that still feels like value even after the post-pandemic surge.
sights: Grand Palace and Wat Phra Kaew, Bangkok — gilded complex that anchors every itinerary
Wat Pho, Bangkok — reclining Buddha 46 m long; oldest and largest temple in the city
Ayutthaya Historical Park — UNESCO royal capital ruins 1.5h north, photogenic at golden hour
Doi Suthep temple, Chiang Mai — hilltop golden chedi overlooking the entire northern valley
Phi Phi Islands (Ko Phi Phi Don and Leh) — turquoise lagoons and Maya Bay from The Beach
Railay Beach, Krabi — car-free limestone peninsula accessible only by longtail boat
activities: Island-hopping and snorkeling by speedboat around Andaman archipelago (Phi Phi, 4 Islands, Phang Nga)
Half-day at an ethical elephant sanctuary (Elephant Nature Park, Chiang Mai) — bathing, no riding
Bangkok food walk: Yaowarat (Chinatown), Ari, and a Michelin-starred street stall dinner
Chao Phraya river and klong (canal) cruise by longtail — temple-hop without queuing on foot
Thai cooking class (half-day) in Bangkok or Chiang Mai: market sourcing plus 4-course cook
Muay Thai evening at Lumpinee or Rajadamnern stadium, Bangkok — ringside seats available

3: Day 1 — Bangkok: Grand Palace, Wat Pho, longtail canal ride, Chao Phraya dinner cruise at dusk
Day 2 — Ayutthaya day trip by train (1.5h); cycle the ruins; return for Asiatique riverside night market
Day 3 — Chatuchak Weekend Market or Jim Thompson House; Yaowarat Chinatown street dinner; depart
5: Day 1 — Bangkok: Grand Palace, Wat Pho, klong canal ride, evening Asiatique floating market
Day 2 — Ayutthaya UNESCO ruins by minivan; return via Bang Pa-In Summer Palace; Sukhumvit dinner
Day 3 — Fly Bangkok to Phuket (1h); settle Karon or Kata Beach; sunset at Promthep Cape
Day 4 — Full-day Phi Phi Islands speedboat tour: Maya Bay, snorkeling, Viking Cave viewpoint
Day 5 — Old Phuket Town street art + Sino-Portuguese heritage walk; Big Buddha; depart Phuket
7: Day 1 — Bangkok: Grand Palace, Wat Pho, Klong Toei fresh market, Asiatique evening
Day 2 — Ayutthaya historical park day trip by train; golden-hour ruins; return to Bangkok
Day 3 — Fly to Chiang Mai (1.25h); Doi Suthep hilltop temple; Nimman Road café crawl and Night Bazaar
Day 4 — Elephant Nature Park half-day (ethical bathing); afternoon Old City temple circuit (Wat Chedi Luang)
Day 5 — Fly Chiang Mai to Krabi or Phuket (1.5h); beach check-in; Ao Nang or Kata evening
Day 6 — Full-day island-hopping: Phi Phi Don + Leh or Krabi 4 Islands; snorkeling, beach BBQ lunch
Day 7 — Morning beach; Old Phuket Town walk; Big Buddha; depart from Phuket International

international.stay: Bangkok: Sukhumvit (BTS-connected, shopping, nightlife) or Old City (temples at doorstep); budget guesthouses ₹1,800–3,500/night, comfort 4-star ₹5,000–10,000, luxury on Chao Phraya ₹18,000–40,000. Phuket: Patong for energy, Karon/Kata for families, Kamala/Surin for luxury quietude; comfort resorts ₹7,000–15,000, five-star pool villas ₹25,000–70,000. Chiang Mai: Old City boutique guesthouses ₹2,500–5,000 or Nimman area design hotels ₹5,000–10,000; luxury teak pool lodges ₹15,000+.
dayTrips: Damnoen Saduak or Amphawa Floating Market — 1.5h drive from Bangkok; best at 7–9am before tour buses
Kanchanaburi and Bridge on the River Kwai — 2h drive; WWII Death Railway, waterfall, quiet guesthouses
James Bond Island (Phang Nga Bay) — 1.5h by speedboat from Phuket; limestone sea caves + kayaking
Krabi and Railay Beach — 1.5h by ferry from Phuket; rock-climbing, four islands, no cars
international.pack: Lightweight breathable cotton (temple cover-ups or sarong essential); reef-safe sunscreen; insect repellent with DEET; stomach rehydration sachets; Grab app installed before departure; power bank for all-day sightseeing.

gettingThere.air: Direct 3–3.5h from Delhi or Mumbai to Bangkok Suvarnabhumi (IndiGo, Air India, Thai Airways, AirAsia); select direct Delhi–Phuket flights ~4.5h; internal Bangkok–Phuket or Bangkok–Chiang Mai ~1–1.25h on AirAsia/Thai Smile (₹3,000–7,000 booked early)
gettingThere.rail: No rail link from India to Thailand; within Bangkok BTS Skytrain and MRT metro cover all tourist zones (single ride ₹30–80); Ayutthaya reachable by Thai Railways from Hua Lamphong station in 1.5h (₹150)
gettingThere.road: No practical overland route from India; within Thailand public minivans connect Bangkok–Pattaya 1.5h (₹200), Bangkok–Kanchanaburi 2h (₹250); inter-city coaches Bangkok–Chiang Mai overnight 9h (₹600)

international.permits: The Thai Cabinet voted on May 19, 2026 to remove Indian passport holders from the visa-exemption list and reclassify them as Visa on Arrival (VOA); the change takes effect 15 days after publication in the Royal Gazette, which had not been confirmed as of late June 2026 — reconfirm the gazette effective date before ticketing. Once in effect, VOA is available at Suvarnabhumi, Phuket, Don Mueang, and select other international ports: the fee is THB 2,000 (~Rs 5,900) payable in Thai Baht cash only at the counter, the maximum stay is 15 days with no extension permitted, and travelers must hold a return or onward ticket departing within those 15 days; proof of funds required is 10,000 THB per person (~Rs 30,000) or 20,000 THB per family. For stays beyond 15 days, a Tourist e-Visa must be applied for at thaievisa.go.th before departure from India: processing takes approximately 14 working days, a single-entry visa costs ~Rs 3,000, and a 6-month multiple-entry costs ~Rs 13,500, both allowing a 60-day stay. The Thailand Digital Arrival Card (TDAC) has been mandatory for all foreign arrivals since May 1, 2025: complete it free at tdac.immigration.go.th within 72 hours of scheduled arrival, which generates a QR code scanned by immigration at the port; airlines can and do deny boarding if the traveler cannot produce TDAC confirmation, and it fully replaces the old paper TM6 form. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months. No inner-line or protected-area permits are required for the standard tourist circuit; Chiang Rai border-zone and hill-tribe villages near the Myanmar border may involve checkpoint registration, which organised tours handle automatically.

operator.costBand: ₹60–90k pp / 5-6 days incl. return flights (economy, advance booking); beaches + city mid-range hotels
operator.whoToSend: Couples on first international trip, families with teens, friend groups mixing culture and beach; also confident solo women (Bangkok and Phuket are very safe and well-signed)
operator.pairsWith: Singapore (4h flight, easy add-on, doubles as transit hub), Bali (same budget tier, contrasting Hindu-beach vibe), Vietnam or Cambodia (Southeast Asia triangle for 10-12 days)

catches: West coast (Phuket, Krabi) is effectively off-season May–Oct due to monsoon, but the east coast (Koh Samui, Koh Phangan) runs its own monsoon Nov–Jan — booking the wrong coast in the wrong month lands a client on a flooded beach.

Songkran (mid-April Thai New Year) turns Bangkok into a citywide water fight: hotels charge 2–3× standard rates, the Grand Palace and Wat Pho are dangerously crowded, and city streets are largely impassable — price it accurately or route clients elsewhere.

All temples enforce the shoulders-and-knees dress code without exception; sarong rental is available at Grand Palace gates but adds roughly 25 minutes to the entry queue, particularly brutal in April heat — brief clients before they leave home.

At Suvarnabhumi, unofficial touts charge Rs 2,000–3,500 for fixed-price rides to the city; official metered taxis in the queued lane outside arrivals cost Rs 600–900 to central Bangkok — advise clients to pre-install Grab and use it from the airport.

Tuk-tuk drivers near Khao San Road routinely tell tourists the Grand Palace is closed that day and offer to take them to a gem shop instead — the palace is almost never actually closed; clients should ignore the claim and walk directly to the entrance.

Once the VOA policy takes effect, Indian arrivals must queue at the separate Visa on Arrival counter at Suvarnabhumi rather than main passport control; this counter routinely runs 1–2 hours during peak morning windows when multiple South Asia and Gulf flights land simultaneously — never book an onward domestic connection within 3 hours of the international arrival.
international.name: Thailand

international.region: Bali, Indonesia · sea level to 1,717 m (Mt Batur)
international.pitch: Imagine mist rising off emerald rice terraces at dawn, a sea-cliff Hindu temple glowing at dusk, and a Balinese massage under the stars — all within one direct flight from India.

climate.note: Peak wet season; daily heavy downpours, flooding, outdoor treks inadvisable
Wettest month of year; persistent rain, rough surf
Rains easing; Nyepi (airport closure day) falls here — check exact date
Transitional; showers tapering, humidity high, uncrowded
Dry season begins; clear skies, manageable crowds
Cooler, dry, excellent visibility for diving and trekking
Peak dry, coolest nights; busiest tourist month — book ahead
Peak dry; festive Galungan/Kuningan temple decorations possible
Still dry, crowds thinning, very comfortable
End of dry; scattered afternoon showers, prices drop
Wet season returns; rain mostly in afternoon bursts
Wet season; Christmas–NYE peak crowds + high hotel rates

international.whyGo: Bali is Indonesia's only Hindu-majority island, producing an unbroken sensory blend of incense-scented temple courtyards, terraced paddy fields, volcanic crater lakes, and Indian Ocean surf that no other island destination replicates. It sells equally well as a honeymoon, a wellness retreat, or a young-group beach-party week — often all three in one trip.
sights: Tanah Lot Sea Temple (offshore rock shrine, sunset icon)
Tegallalang Rice Terraces, Ubud (UNESCO-nominated subak irrigation)
Uluwatu Cliff Temple (70-m sea cliff, kecak fire dance arena)
Mount Batur active volcano & crater lake (1,717 m, sunrise trek)
Tirta Empul Holy Spring Temple (ritual purification bathing pools)
Sacred Monkey Forest Sanctuary, Ubud (ancient temples, 700+ macaques)
activities: Sunrise trek to Mount Batur crater rim — 4-hr round trip, guides mandatory
White-water rafting on Ayung River, Ubud (Grade II–III, beginner-friendly)
Balinese cooking class (wet-market tour to 5-dish meal) in Ubud
Surf lesson on Kuta or Seminyak beach (2-hr beginner session ~USD 25)
Kecak fire dance at Uluwatu — 6 pm performance, book afternoon cliff walk combo
Traditional Balinese four-hand massage and floral-bath ritual at a Ubud spa

3: Arrive Denpasar (DPS); transfer Seminyak; check in, sunset puja walk at Tanah Lot temple
Ubud full day: Tegallalang rice terraces, Sacred Monkey Forest, Tirta Empul holy spring dip
Uluwatu cliff temple walk + kecak fire dance at dusk; Jimbaran Bay grilled-seafood dinner; fly out
5: Arrive DPS; Seminyak beach settle-in; sunset at Tanah Lot
Ubud: Tegallalang rice terraces, Monkey Forest, Saraswati water palace, art market browse
Pre-dawn Mount Batur volcano trek to crater rim (1,717 m); soak at Toya Devasya hot springs; Kintamani crater views
Uluwatu sea-cliff walk + 6 pm kecak fire dance; Jimbaran Bay seafood dinner
Morning Balinese spa; Seminyak boutique shopping; afternoon fly out

Kuta/Seminyak beach day; 2-hr surf lesson; sunset at beach club
Ubud: Tegallalang terraces, Monkey Forest, Saraswati palace, gallery walk, night market
Sunrise hike Mount Batur crater rim; Tirta Empul ritual bath; Kintamani lunch with caldera view
Uluwatu cliff temple; 6 pm kecak fire dance; Jimbaran Bay grilled-seafood dinner on the sand
Day trip Nusa Penida: Kelingking T-Rex cliff viewpoint, Angel's Billabong, snorkel Crystal Bay
Morning four-hand Balinese massage; Seminyak market; fly home

international.stay: Seminyak/Legian: nightlife, beach clubs, boutique villas — budget ₹3,000/night to luxury ₹18,000/night. Ubud: cultural heart, rice-field infinity-pool villas, wellness retreats — mid ₹4,000 to luxury ₹25,000. Jimbaran/Nusa Dua: 5-star resort corridor, calm beaches, best for families. Canggu: surfer-digital-nomad zone, mid-range cafes, artsy crowd.
dayTrips: Nusa Penida (45-min speedboat from Sanur piers): Kelingking cliff, Angel's Billabong, manta-ray snorkel — full day, book boat + guide combo ~USD 35 pp
Gili Trawangan (2.5-hr fast boat from Padang Bai): white-sand atoll, no motorized vehicles, snorkeling — ideal overnight add-on
Lovina Beach, North Bali (2.5 hrs by road): sunrise dolphin-watching boat, black-sand beach, Banjar hot springs
Lombok (20-min flight or 2.5-hr fast ferry): quieter beaches, Sasak villages, Rinjani base — good 1-night extension
international.pack: Reef-safe sunscreen (mandatory in marine parks), sarong + sash for temples (provided free at most entrances but bring your own), DEET insect repellent, waterproof sandals + lace-up trekking shoes for Batur, universal adapter (Bali uses C/F sockets), USD cash for VoA at airport.

gettingThere.air: IndiGo, Air India, Batik Air fly direct Mumbai/Delhi to Denpasar (DPS) — 5.5 hrs from South India, 6.5–7 hrs from North India; fares ₹18,000–38,000 return. One-stop via Singapore (SIN) or Kuala Lumpur (KUL) adds 2–3 hrs but can be cheaper off-peak.
gettingThere.rail: Not applicable — Bali is an island; no rail link from India or within Bali.
gettingThere.road: Not applicable — island destination; accessible only by air or (from Java/Lombok) short ferry crossing.

international.permits: Indian passport holders enter on Visa on Arrival (VoA) at Ngurah Rai International Airport; the fee is IDR 500,000 (~USD 35 / ~₹3,000), payable by card or foreign-currency cash (USD, EUR, AUD, GBP, SGD accepted; INR is not accepted). A separate mandatory Bali Tourism Levy of IDR 150,000 (~USD 10), in force since 14 February 2024, applies to all foreign visitors and is cashless only — pay online before departure at lovebali.baliprov.go.id, which issues a QR-code voucher by email; total entry cost is therefore ~USD 45. Alternatively, apply for an e-VoA before departure at evisa.imigrasi.go.id or the VFS Global Indonesia portal (same IDR 500,000 fee, 30-day validity, email approval within 24–72 hours), which allows clients to bypass the airport VoA queue entirely. Both VoA and e-VoA permit a 30-day stay, extendable once for a further 30 days (IDR 500,000 / ~USD 35) at an Indonesian immigration office in person; since June 2025, all extensions require biometric collection (fingerprints and photo) under Circular Letter IMI-417.GR.01.01/2025 — online renewal is no longer accepted. Sarong and sash are mandatory at all temples and are supplied free at most entrances (occasionally charged a ₹50–100 equivalent). Nyepi Day 2026 falls 19 March 6:00 AM to 20 March 6:00 AM: Ngurah Rai Airport closes completely for 24 hours and all outdoor movement is prohibited island-wide — the date shifts each year, so reconfirm before quoting any March itinerary.

operator.costBand: ₹75,000–1,10,000 pp / 5–6 days (return flights from Mumbai or Delhi, 3-star hotel or mid-range villa, airport transfers, 2 guided day tours included)
operator.whoToSend: Honeymooners and couples (top segment), young groups (beach-party + adventure mix), solo wellness/yoga travellers, families with children 10+; avoid for elderly clients with mobility issues due to uneven temple steps
operator.pairsWith: Singapore (3-night stopover en route — sells easily as twin-city), Lombok + Gili Islands (7+3 split), Komodo National Park (liveaboard add-on for divers)

catches: Entry costs ~USD 45, not ~USD 35: the VoA fee (IDR 500,000) and the mandatory Bali Tourism Levy (IDR 150,000, introduced 14 February 2024) are two separate payments — the levy is cashless only, best settled at lovebali.baliprov.go.id before departure; INR is not accepted for the VoA fee.

Clients can skip the airport VoA queue by applying for an e-VoA before departure at evisa.imigrasi.go.id or the VFS Global Indonesia portal (IDR 500,000, 30-day stay, email approval within 24–72 hours).

Since June 2025, stay extensions require an in-person visit to an Indonesian immigration office for biometric collection (fingerprints and photo) under Circular Letter IMI-417.GR.01.01/2025; online extensions are no longer accepted.

Nyepi Day 2026 (19 March 6:00 AM – 20 March 6:00 AM) shuts Ngurah Rai Airport for 24 hours and bans all outdoor movement island-wide; the date shifts each year — reconfirm before booking any March itinerary.
December–February monsoon brings daily 2–4 hour downpours that flood Ubud lanes and make the Batur trek dangerous; reframe those days around spa or cooking-class activities, or rebook.

In Kuta and temple zones, instruct clients to download Grab or Gojek before landing (fares run 5–8× lower than tout prices); at Kuta and Dreamland beaches, clients should swim only between red-and-yellow lifeguard flags and never at unpatrolled spots after dark due to unpredictable rip currents.
international.name: Bali (Indonesia)

international.region: Vietnam · Sea level to 3,143 m (Fansipan); multi-region North–Central–South
international.pitch: Vietnam is three countries in one itinerary — ancient Hanoi alleys, Halong Bay's limestone theatre, and Hoi An lanterns — all at a price that makes five-star feel budget.

climate.note: North cool and dry; South peak dry season; perfect nationwide
Best weather countrywide; Tet (late Jan/Feb) causes price spikes and closures
Warming but dry; cherry blossoms in Sa Pa highlands
Hot in south; North comfortable; pre-monsoon humidity building
South wet season starts; North rains begin; still workable with flexibility
Monsoon across North and Central; Halong Bay tours frequently cancelled
Peak monsoon; typhoon risk on Central coast; flooding in Ha Giang
Heaviest rains; typhoons; Sa Pa roads may close; Halong rough seas
Typhoon season Central coast; North floods ease late month
South dry season begins; Central Vietnam (Hoi An/Hue) at flood peak — avoid
North and South excellent; Hoi An lantern festival; Sa Pa harvest gold
Peak season North and South; cool nights in Hanoi; crowded but optimal

international.whyGo: Vietnam delivers epic visual range — karst bays, terraced highlands, imperial citadels, colonial beach towns — compressed into a flyable 1,650 km strip. Street food culture rivals any on earth, and mid-range hospitality punches well above its price tag for Indian travellers on 6-10 day breaks.
sights: Halong Bay — 1,600 limestone karsts rising from emerald water
Hoi An Ancient Town — UNESCO lantern-lit trading port
Hue Imperial Citadel and royal tombs
Phong Nha-Ke Bang caves (Son Doong / Paradise Cave)
Sa Pa rice terraces and Fansipan summit
Ho Chi Minh City — War Remnants Museum, Ben Thanh, cu chi tunnels
activities: 2-night Halong Bay cruise — kayaking sea caves, floating villages, sunrise on deck
Ha Giang motorbike loop — 350 km mountain circuit through Dong Van karst plateau
Hoi An cooking class — market tour, clay pot fish, fresh spring rolls
Phong Nha cave kayak through Paradise or Dark Cave
Fansipan cable-car + short summit trek (3,143 m, takes 1 day)
Grab-bike street food crawl in Hanoi Old Quarter — bun cha, banh mi, egg coffee

3: Hanoi: Old Quarter walk, Hoan Kiem Lake, Temple of Literature, egg coffee, bun cha dinner
Halong Bay overnight cruise: limestone karsts, kayaking sea caves, floating village, sunset drinks
Return Hanoi by noon: quick Ninh Binh pagoda stop or same-day flight south
5: Arrive Hanoi: Old Quarter, Thang Long Water Puppets, bia hoi corner
Halong Bay cruise: kayak sea caves, fishing village, sunrise on deck
Fly Hanoi→Da Nang; transfer to Hoi An; Ancient Town, lantern riverside dinner
Hoi An: tailors, cooking class, My Son Cham ruins afternoon
Da Nang: Marble Mountains, Dragon Bridge; evening flight home
7: Arrive Hanoi: Old Quarter, Hoan Kiem, Thang Long Water Puppets, bun cha
Halong Bay cruise day 1: karst kayak, floating village, cave walk
Halong return Hanoi; afternoon flight south to Da Nang; Hoi An check-in
Hoi An: Ancient Town, tailors, white rose dumplings, lantern release at dusk
Hue day trip: Imperial Citadel, Thien Mu Pagoda, royal tomb cycle, lemongrass beef
Fly to HCMC: Cu Chi Tunnels, Ben Thanh Market, rooftop bar District 1
Mekong Delta day tour: Cai Rang floating market, coconut candy factory, sampan canals

international.stay: Hanoi: Old Quarter (budget guesthouses ₹1,800–3,500/night; boutique heritage ₹5,000–9,000; West Lake luxury ₹12,000+). Hoi An: Ancient Town fringe (mid-range ₹4,000–8,000) or An Bang beach resort strip (₹9,000–22,000). HCMC: District 1 covers all budgets (budget ₹2,000–4,000; luxury Reverie/Park Hyatt ₹18,000+). Sa Pa: eco-lodge ₹6,000–14,000 with valley views.
dayTrips: Ninh Binh / Trang An (2 h south of Hanoi) — boat through limestone river gorges, Bai Dinh pagoda
Sa Pa (5 h Hanoi by overnight train or 4.5 h new expressway) — rice terrace trek, Hmong villages
Phong Nha caves (4 h south of Hue by hired car) — Paradise or Dark Cave, river kayak
Phu Quoc island (1 h flight from HCMC) — beaches, night market, snorkel reefs; easy 2-night add-on
international.pack: Light breathable cotton or linen (high humidity), packable rain jacket, sturdy walking sandals, modest cover-up (shoulders + knees) for temples, mosquito repellent with DEET, powerbank, VND cash (₹1 = ~290 VND; street stalls rarely accept card).

gettingThere.air: IndiGo / Vietnam Airlines Delhi→Hanoi ~5 h direct (daily); Mumbai→HCMC ~5.5 h via Singapore or Bangkok (Vietnam Airlines, Vistara codeshare, Air Asia); return fares ₹18,000–38,000. Book 6–8 weeks out for Nov–Feb peak. Internal one-way flights (Hanoi↔Da Nang↔HCMC) on VietJet/Bamboo ₹1,800–5,500.
gettingThere.rail: No rail link from India. Within Vietnam, Reunification Express Hanoi→HCMC covers 1,726 km in ~30 h; sleeper berths ₹1,200–2,800; scenic Hanoi→Da Nang segment (~16 h, overnight) worth it once.
gettingThere.road: No viable road route from India. Within Vietnam, open-top tourist buses (The Sinh Tourist, Phuong Trang) link Hanoi–Hue–Hoi An–HCMC; budget option, 10–14 h per leg.

international.permits: Indian passport holders require a Vietnamese e-visa; no visa-free entry or visa on arrival is available. Apply at evisa.gov.vn (the current official portal since 11 November 2024); processing takes 3–5 business days, so apply before departure. Single-entry costs US $25 (~₹2,100); multiple-entry costs US $50 (~₹4,200); the visa is valid for up to 90 days. Standard routes (Hanoi, Halong Bay, Hoi An, HCMC) require no additional permits. The Ha Giang Loop sits in a border zone: all foreign visitors must collect a border-area travel permit ("Giấy phép vào khu vực biên giới") at the immigration/police office in Ha Giang City before setting off — not at a roadside checkpoint — at approximately VND 200,000–250,000 (~US $10 pp, same-day issuance on passport presentation); self-riders additionally need a valid home-country motorcycle licence and a 1968-convention International Driving Permit (IDP), as enforcement is now active and fines are significant. Son Doong cave is operated exclusively by Oxalis Adventure under an annual quota of 1,000 visitors; as of mid-2026 all tours are fully booked through 2027, making the realistic lead time 18 months to 2+ years ahead; price is approximately US $3,000 pp (VND 79,500,000) — reconfirm availability and open booking windows before quoting clients.

operator.costBand: ₹70,000–1,20,000 pp / 7 days incl. return flights (Delhi/Mumbai); budget 5-day North-only trips possible at ₹50,000–65,000 pp
operator.whoToSend: Couples (honeymoon and anniversary), solo millennial travellers, food-focused groups, adventure riders (Ha Giang), families with teens comfortable with long-haul Southeast Asia
operator.pairsWith: Cambodia (Angkor Wat, 3–4 day add-on via HCMC); Thailand (Bangkok + Chiang Mai or Phuket via same flight hub); Laos (Luang Prabang, 3 days, overlaps Hanoi gateway)

catches: Hoi An and Hue flood severely October–November from typhoon runoff; book only with travel insurance and fully flexible/refundable hotels in that window.
Halong Bay has a serious quality gap: boats under US $80/night carry poor safety records; require mid-tier licensed operators such as Paradise, Indochina Junk, or Heritage Line.

Tet (late Jan/Feb) shuts restaurants, museums, and transport for 4–7 days and closes government offices and banks for the full 7-day public holiday; prices triple — book 3–4 months ahead or route clients around this window entirely.
Grab is the only safe ride option in Hanoi Old Quarter and HCMC; taxi and cyclo meter scams are rampant, and Grab saves 40–60% while eliminating meter fraud.
Ha Giang Loop roads close unpredictably after heavy rain; verify road status at the guesthouse each morning before riding, and consider booking a local Easy Rider guide to reduce risk materially.

Any itinerary that exits Vietnam to Laos, Cambodia, or China requires the multiple-entry e-visa (US $50) selected at application time; a single-entry visa (US $25) cannot be re-used and requires a full new application to re-enter Vietnam.

international.region: Maldives · Indian Ocean archipelago · sea level
international.pitch: Picture waking up in a glass-floor overwater villa with a manta ray gliding past below — the Maldives is the one destination where the honeymoon fantasy is the literal reality.

climate.note: Peak dry season; gin-clear water, calm seas, full resort availability
Best underwater visibility; most expensive month, book 4-6 months ahead
Hot and sunny; whale sharks peak in South Ari Atoll
Heat spikes; seas still mostly calm but SW monsoon approaching
SW monsoon onset; choppy transfers, patchy visibility, some closures
Monsoon peak; heavy squalls, rough inter-atoll seas
Rough seas; manta rays gather in Baa Atoll but transfers uncomfortable
Wettest month; strong winds, low water clarity
Monsoon persists; occasional sunny spells but seas unpredictable
Monsoon easing; Hanifaru Bay manta ray season peaks, good for dive deals

Peak season; Christmas-New Year adds 20-30% surcharge

international.whyGo: The Maldives is the world's flattest country — 1,200 coral islands across 26 atolls — where the only agenda is turquoise lagoons, house reefs teeming with sharks and rays, and overwater architecture that makes every room feel like an aquarium. For Indian travellers it is the closest true luxury-island escape, reachable in under four hours from most metros.
sights: Male — Hukuru Miskiy coral-stone mosque (1658), colourful fish market and waterfront
Baa Atoll — UNESCO Biosphere Reserve; Hanifaru Bay aggregates hundreds of manta rays Jun-Nov
South Ari Atoll — year-round whale shark aggregation site; world's most reliable encounter
Maafushi — thriving local island 45 min from Male; budget guesthouses, bikini beach, street food
Vaadhoo Island — bioluminescent plankton beach (phytoplankton sea of stars at night)
Hulhumale — reclaimed island adjacent to Velana airport; coral reefs, cheap eats, day-use base
activities: Snorkel or dive with whale sharks in South Ari Atoll (best Jan-May, present year-round)
Sunrise breakfast on a private sandbank reached by dhoni boat
Sunset dolphin-watching cruise on a traditional Maldivian dhow
PADI Open Water dive certification in a warm, calm resort lagoon
Night dive or bioluminescent beach walk at Vaadhoo or Reethi Beach
Seaplane scenic flight between atolls — aerial view of the reef ring-and-lagoon system

3: Day 1: Fly into Velana International; speedboat transfer to North Male Atoll resort (45 min); settle in; sunset dolphin cruise by dhoni
Day 2: Dawn house-reef snorkel; glass-bottom boat tour over coral gardens; afternoon sandbank picnic
Day 3: Overwater spa session; water sports; speedboat back to Male by 14:00; evening departure flight
5: Day 1: Arrive Male; seaplane transfer to Ari Atoll resort (25 min scenic flight); lagoon swim and resort orientation
Day 2: All-day whale shark excursion in South Ari — snorkel alongside 5-8 m individuals with naturalist guide
Day 3: PADI discover-scuba dive on house reef; afternoon guided turtle and reef-shark snorkel
Day 4: Sandbank breakfast picnic; Dhigurah local island visit — fishing village, Friday mosque; sunset catamaran
Day 5: Morning kayaking; pack and seaplane back to Male; afternoon departure
7: Day 1: Arrive Velana; Male half-day walk — fish market, Hukuru Miskiy; speedboat to North Male Atoll resort
Day 2: House reef snorkel at dawn; glass-bottom boat; sunset dolphin cruise
Day 3: Seaplane transfer to Baa Atoll eco-resort (30 min); afternoon lagoon kayak and orientation
Day 4: Hanifaru Bay manta ray snorkel with marine biologist (UNESCO protected excursion, 6-person limit)
Day 5: Seaplane to Ari Atoll; settle in new resort; afternoon reef dive
Day 6: Full-day whale shark excursion in South Ari; evening bioluminescent beach walk
Day 7: Morning water sports and spa; speedboat to Male; evening international departure

international.stay: North Male Atoll (30-90 min speedboat from airport): widest range from ₹12k/night boutique guesthouses to ₹1.2L/night overwater villas — best for short trips. Ari Atoll (25 min seaplane): mid-range to luxury; go here for wildlife. Baa Atoll (30 min seaplane): eco-luxury only; best Jun-Nov for mantas. Local islands (Maafushi, Dhigurah): guesthouses ₹4,000-8,000/night pp; Islamic modesty rules apply outside designated bikini beaches; no alcohol.
dayTrips: Male city half-day — Hukuru Miskiy, fish market, Sultan Park museum; 30-45 min speedboat from most North Male resorts
Maafushi local island — house reef snorkel, street-food lunch, meet locals; 45 min speedboat from Male jetty
Semi-submersible or submarine tour from Male — reef exploration without getting wet; departs Hulhumale pier daily
Sri Lanka 3-night extension — Colombo or Sigiriya heritage circuit; 2.5 h direct flight from Male on SriLankan Airlines
international.pack: Light cottons and swimwear, full-body rash guard (reef sun is intense), reef-safe mineral sunscreen, GoPro or underwater housing, one modest long-sleeve cover-up for local island visits, motion-sickness tablets for speedboat transfers in choppy season.

gettingThere.air: Direct flights from Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai, Kochi to Velana International (Male) on IndiGo, SpiceJet, Air India, GoAir — 3 to 4 h; then speedboat 30-90 min or seaplane 20-45 min to resort (seaplanes daytime only)
gettingThere.rail: No rail connection — not applicable
gettingThere.road: No road connection — island nation; not applicable

international.permits: Indian passport holders receive a free 30-day visa on arrival at Velana International Airport; no advance application or fee is required. The Maldives Immigration minimum passport validity requirement is 1 month from the date of arrival; airlines independently enforce their own 6-month norm at check-in, so travelers with fewer than 6 months remaining should confirm with their carrier before travel. Entry requires a confirmed return ticket and a resort or hotel booking confirmation; Maldives immigration formally requires proof of at least USD 100 per person per day of stay, though this is rarely checked for guests presenting resort bookings. All visitors must complete the free IMUGA Traveller Declaration at imuga.immigration.gov.mv within 96 hours before arrival — non-submission can result in denied boarding at the origin airport. No inner-line or protected-area permits are needed for standard atoll travel. Entry to Hanifaru Bay in Baa Atoll requires a park entry fee of USD 20–30 per person paid at the ranger station; reconfirm the current rate before quoting, as it has risen from earlier figures.

operator.costBand: ₹80k–1.5L pp / 5-6 nights mid-range resort (incl. return flights from metro); budget local-island stay ₹50-70k pp; luxury overwater villa ₹2.5L+ pp
operator.whoToSend: Honeymooners, anniversary couples, families with older teens wanting pure beach luxury with zero logistics stress; works for anyone who has already done a hill-station honeymoon and wants the international upgrade
operator.pairsWith: Sri Lanka (2.5 h from Male — add Colombo or Sigiriya), Dubai (Emirates stopover on return), Kochi (southern India gateway, convenient pre-flight night)

catches: Trans Maldivian Airways (the dominant seaplane operator) requires passengers to land at Velana by 15:30 for any same-day resort transfer — a 16:00 arrival is as stranding as a midnight one, and many afternoon flights from India fall after this cutoff, forcing a Male overnight; budget ₹5,000–10,000 extra for Hulhumale bridge-linked hotels or the Hulhule Island Hotel.

The IMUGA Traveller Declaration is mandatory for all visitors including Indians — it must be submitted free at imuga.immigration.gov.mv within 96 hours before arrival, and airlines can deny boarding or Maldives immigration can deny entry for non-compliance.
All resort pricing is quoted in USD; at 2024–25 exchange rates a $600/night villa runs approximately ₹50,000/night, so packages that look affordable in rupees can produce a sharp final bill.

Alcohol and swimwear are prohibited on local inhabited islands under Maldivian law — itineraries that mix local-island guesthouses with resort stays need careful sequencing to avoid a dry, cover-up-mandatory transit leg.
Excursions — whale shark dives, manta snorkelling, sandbank visits, dolphin cruises — are almost always à-la-carte add-ons that can add ₹15,000–30,000 per person above a stated all-inclusive rate.

The May–October south-west monsoon brings rough inter-atoll speedboat transfers, reduced underwater visibility, and occasional resort closures — advise customers explicitly before they book on the strength of discounted fares alone.
international.name: Maldives

international.region: Sri Lanka · Sea level to 2,524 m (central highlands)
international.pitch: Ancient rock fortresses, leopard safaris, misty tea estates, and turquoise beaches — all packed onto one island just two hours from India.

climate.note: Peak dry season; west and south coasts pristine, wildlife excellent
Clearest skies; best for beaches, whale watching, and cultural sites
Hot and dry; all circuits open, visibility excellent
Pre-monsoon heat spike; afternoon showers, still manageable
SW monsoon arrives; west and south coasts rain-lashed
SW monsoon peak; rough seas, beach activity largely suspended
Monsoon continues; Arugam Bay east coast opens for surfing
SW monsoon; east-coast circuit (Trincomalee, Nilaveli) viable
SW monsoon tapering; west coast still unreliable
Inter-monsoon; brief unpredictable showers on both coasts
NE monsoon starts; north and east coast get wet, west settling
Dry season returns; peak crowds and prices, book well ahead

international.whyGo: Sri Lanka compresses 2,500 years of Buddhist civilisation, leopard-dense jungle, rolling tea highlands, and some of Asia's finest colonial-era beaches into an island smaller than Andhra Pradesh. The payoff-to-effort ratio for Indian travellers is unmatched — it is international travel with a domestic-flight commute.
sights: Sigiriya Rock Fortress (5th-century sky palace, UNESCO)
Temple of the Tooth Relic, Kandy (holiest Buddhist shrine in Sri Lanka)
Dambulla Cave Temples (153 statues across 5 caves, UNESCO)
Galle Fort (intact Dutch colonial walled city, UNESCO)
Yala National Park (world's highest leopard density)
Polonnaruwa ruins (12th-century royal city, UNESCO)
activities: Dawn jeep safari in Yala for leopards, elephants, and sloth bears
Climb Sigiriya Rock at sunrise before the heat and crowds arrive
Ella–Kandy scenic train ride through Nine Arch Bridge and tea estates
Whale watching off Mirissa (blue and sperm whales, Dec–Apr)
Tea estate walk and factory tour in Nuwara Eliya or Haputale
Surfing or snorkelling at Arugam Bay or Hikkaduwa

3: Day 1: Fly Colombo; transfer to Dambulla (3 hr); Cave Temples late afternoon; overnight Sigiriya area
Day 2: Sigiriya Rock climb at sunrise; Polonnaruwa ruins by afternoon; overnight Kandy
Day 3: Temple of the Tooth morning puja; Peradeniya Botanical Gardens; drive Colombo and fly home
5: Day 1: Arrive Colombo; drive to Sigiriya (4 hr); Dambulla Caves en route
Day 2: Sigiriya Rock at dawn; Polonnaruwa ancient city; overnight Kandy
Day 3: Kandy spice market; scenic train Kandy to Nuwara Eliya through highlands; tea estate tour
Day 4: Train to Ella; Nine Arch Bridge; Little Adam's Peak hike; Ravana Falls
Day 5: Drive Galle (4 hr); Fort walk; Unawatuna beach; evening flight Colombo
7: Day 1: Arrive Colombo; Galle Face Green; drive Sigiriya (4 hr)
Day 2: Sigiriya Rock at dawn; Dambulla Cave Temples; Minneriya elephant gathering safari
Day 3: Polonnaruwa ruins; drive to Kandy; Temple of the Tooth evening puja
Day 4: Kandy city; scenic train through highlands to Nuwara Eliya; Mackwoods tea estate
Day 5: Train Ella; Nine Arch Bridge; Little Adam's Peak; Ravana Falls; overnight Ella
Day 6: Drive to Yala (3.5 hr); full-day jeep safari — leopard, elephant, sloth bear
Day 7: Drive Galle (2 hr); Fort colonial quarter; Mirissa beach sunset; Colombo fly home

international.stay: Colombo (transit and city; upscale Cinnamon Grand or boutique Galle Face Hotel belt); Sigiriya area (eco-lodges and jungle camps; Wild Grass, Habarana village); Kandy (heritage hill hotels; Earl's Regency, Theva Residency); Nuwara Eliya or Ella (converted planter's bungalows; Amaya Hills, 98 Acres); Galle Fort (boutique colonial; Amangalla, Fort Bazaar). Budget guesthouses ₹2,500–4,500/night; mid-range boutique ₹7,000–15,000; luxury (Aman Galle, Ceylon Tea Trails) ₹40,000+.
dayTrips: Colombo base → Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage (2.5 hr drive, half-day)
Kandy base → Dambulla Cave Temples + Matale spice gardens (1.5 hr, easy morning)
Galle base → Mirissa whale watching boat (1 hr drive + 3 hr cruise, Dec–Apr)
Ella base → Ravana Falls + Buduruwagala rock carvings (30–45 min, add-on afternoon)
international.pack: Lightweight cotton clothing; reef shoes for rocky beaches; modest temple cover-up (shoulders and knees covered); compact rain jacket (Oct–Nov or if travelling May–Sep east coast); reef-safe sunscreen; ETA printout and USD cash for park fees.

gettingThere.air: Daily direct flights from Chennai (1.5 hr), Bangalore (1.75 hr), Mumbai (2.5 hr), Delhi (3.5 hr) to Colombo Bandaranaike CMB on IndiGo, SpiceJet, Air India, SriLankan Airlines; fares ₹6,000–18,000 return depending on origin and season
gettingThere.rail: No India–Sri Lanka rail link; within Sri Lanka the Kandy–Ella–Colombo scenic rail is a highlight — Kandy to Ella takes ~7 hr, book 2nd-class reserved (₹250) well in advance
gettingThere.road: No road link from India; intermittent Rameshwaram–Talaimannar ferry is not reliably operational — do not plan around it; within Sri Lanka, private A/C van hire ₹4,000–6,000/day covers 200–250 km comfortably

international.permits: As of 2026, Indian passport holders can enter Sri Lanka with only a valid passport (minimum six-month validity) and a confirmed return or onward ticket — no ETA is required and the fee is zero, a policy confirmed by Sri Lanka's High Commissioner to India in February 2026. Because some airline check-in systems still flag a missing ETA, applying for the free ETA at eta.gov.lk at least 48 hours before departure is advisable insurance; processing takes 24–48 hours, and same-day applications carry genuine boarding-denial risk. Authorized stay is 30 days, double-entry. As a SAARC member nation, Indians pay roughly half the standard foreigner rate at every Cultural Triangle heritage site: Sigiriya USD 20, Polonnaruwa USD 15, and Dambulla approximately USD 9–10 at current LKR exchange rates; always present the Indian passport at the CCF ticket counter to claim this rate, and Galle Fort entry is free. SAARC rates also apply at national parks: Yala entry for Indians is USD 15 (off-peak) to USD 20 (peak) plus approximately 15% government tax, with jeep hire paid separately as an additional cost. No inner-line or protected-area permits are required for standard tourist circuits.

operator.costBand: ₹55–90k pp / 5–7 days incl. return flights ex-South India (Chennai, Bangalore); ₹70–1,05k pp from Delhi or Mumbai
operator.whoToSend: Honeymooners and couples (culture-beach combo); wildlife enthusiasts (Yala leopards); families with school-age children; first-time international travellers from South India seeking easy visa and short flights
operator.pairsWith: Maldives 4-night add-on (same flight corridor, combine into a 10-day package); Kerala backwaters pre- or post-trip (fly Kochi–Colombo, land Trivandrum)

catches: As of 2026, Sri Lanka's High Commissioner to India confirmed Indians need no ETA, but other official Sri Lankan sources and some airline check-in systems still require one — apply for the free ETA at eta.gov.lk at least 48 hours before departure, as processing takes 24–48 hours and same-day applications carry genuine boarding-denial risk regardless of the official waiver.

The SAARC discount is frequently missing from client quotes: Indians pay Sigiriya USD 20 (not USD 30), Polonnaruwa USD 15 (not USD 30), and Dambulla approximately USD 10 (not USD 15); at Yala the Indian rate is USD 15–20 plus ~15% government tax versus USD 40 for non-SAARC visitors, making the all-in Yala day (entry + tax + shared jeep) roughly USD 50–80 per person, not USD 90–120 — always present the Indian passport at ticket counters.

The southwest monsoon (May–September) closes west and south coast beaches entirely; pivoting to the east coast (Arugam Bay, Trincomalee) requires redesigning the full itinerary, not just swapping a hotel.

Road times are far longer than map distances suggest: Colombo to Ella is 300 km but takes six to seven hours on mountain roads, and over-scheduled day-by-day itineraries are the most common client complaint.

Kandy Esala Perahera (late July to mid-August) drives hotel rates two to three times above normal across the entire Cultural Triangle — book at least six months ahead or reroute the cultural-triangle segment to avoid the peak.
international.name: Sri Lanka

international.region: Dubai · UAE · sea level
international.pitch: From a gold souk on a creek to the world's tallest tower and a Bedouin camp under desert stars — Dubai delivers a decade of "firsts" inside four days.

climate.note: peak season; clear skies, cool evenings
peak season; occasional dust haze, still perfect
warm days, last truly comfortable month
heating up; mornings manageable, afternoons sticky
hot; outdoor sightseeing uncomfortable by 10 am
extreme heat + humidity; mostly indoors-only trip
peak summer; 90%+ humidity, brutal even at night
peak summer heat; viable only for mall-hopping
still very hot; slight relief only after sunset
cooling; bearable outdoors by evening, good deals
shoulder-peak; excellent weather, pre-holiday prices
peak; NYE fireworks pack the city — book 6–8 wks ahead

international.whyGo: Dubai is the ultimate first-international-trip destination for Indian travellers — walkable visa process, Hindi widely understood, and a skyline that fuses Blade Runner with Arabian Nights. It layers serious luxury alongside surprisingly affordable street food and free beaches, meaning it works for both budget-extenders and big spenders on the same tour.
sights: Burj Khalifa (At the Top SKY, 148F)
Dubai Frame — framed views of old and new city
Al Fahidi Historic District + Dubai Museum
Gold Souk and Spice Souk, Deira
Palm Jumeirah — The View at The Palm monorail loop
Dubai Creek — abra water taxi crossing
activities: Desert safari — dune bashing, sandboarding, camel ride, falconry, Bedouin camp dinner
Dhow dinner cruise on Dubai Creek or Marina
Burj Khalifa observation deck at sunset or sunrise
Atlantis Aquaventure Waterpark (Palm Jumeirah)
Dubai Miracle Garden (Oct–Apr only) and Global Village (seasonal)
Dubai Mall — Dubai Aquarium, ice rink, fountain show at 18:00 and 21:30 nightly

3: Day 1: Dubai Mall on arrival, Burj Khalifa At the Top at sunset (book online 48 hr ahead), Dubai Fountain show dinner at Souk Al Bahar
Day 2: Al Fahidi Historic District, Dubai Museum, abra to Gold Souk and Spice Souk, evening dhow dinner cruise on Creek
Day 3: Morning desert safari (departs 14:00) — dune bashing, camel ride, sandboarding, Bedouin camp BBQ dinner under stars
5: Day 1: Arrive, Dubai Marina walk, JBR beachfront dinner
Day 2: Burj Khalifa At the Top SKY (148F), Dubai Mall, Fountain show
Day 3: Al Fahidi, Dubai Museum, Creek abra, Gold + Spice Souks, evening dhow cruise
Day 4: Palm Jumeirah monorail, The View at The Palm, Atlantis Aquaventure waterpark
Day 5: Evening desert safari with dune bashing and Bedouin camp; late-night depart or next-day flight
7: Day 1: Arrive; JBR beach sunset walk, Dubai Marina dinner cruise
Day 2: Burj Khalifa At the Top SKY, Dubai Mall, Aquarium, Dubai Fountain
Day 3: Al Fahidi Historic District, Dubai Museum, Creek abra, Gold + Spice Souks, Deira street food
Day 4: Palm Jumeirah — The View at The Palm, Atlantis Aquaventure, beachfront sundowner
Day 5: Desert safari — dune bashing, quad biking, falconry, Bedouin camp with live music
Day 6: Day trip Abu Dhabi — Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, Louvre Abu Dhabi or Ferrari World (140 km, 1.5 hr drive)
Day 7: Dubai Frame, Miracle Garden (seasonal Oct–Apr), Global Village if running; depart

international.stay: Downtown Dubai (Emaar strip — closest to Burj Khalifa/Mall; comfort to luxury ₹8,000–25,000/night); Dubai Marina/JBR (beach-adjacent, lively nightlife, comfort ₹6,500–18,000/night); Deira (budget enclave, ₹3,000–6,000/night, great for souk access and Indian food strips on Al Rigga Road); Palm Jumeirah (Atlantis, Waldorf, Raffles — full luxury experience, ₹18,000–60,000/night).
dayTrips: Abu Dhabi (140 km, 1.5 hr): Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque + Louvre Abu Dhabi — easy full-day add-on
Sharjah (25 km, 30 min): Blue Souk, Heritage Area, Sharjah Art Museum — strict dry emirate, dress conservatively
Hatta (100 km, 1 hr): Hatta Dam kayaking, mountain bike trails, Hatta Heritage Village — cooler in winters
Fujairah/Khor Fakkan (150 km, 1.5 hr): East coast snorkelling, Al Hajar mountains, Snoopy Island diving
international.pack: Light cotton/linen for outdoor heat, one modest outfit (covered shoulders + knees) for souks/malls, swimwear for beach/waterpark, comfortable walking shoes for souk lanes; light jacket for over-airconditioned malls and desert nights (Dec–Feb can drop to 12°C).

gettingThere.air: Direct daily flights from Mumbai (3 hr), Delhi (3.5 hr), Bengaluru/Chennai/Hyderabad/Kochi (~3–3.5 hr), Ahmedabad (3 hr) on Emirates, IndiGo, Air Arabia, SpiceJet, Air India; return fares ₹12,000–30,000 depending on season and airline; Nov–Jan fares peak, May–Sep cheapest
gettingThere.rail: No rail link — not applicable for international travel
gettingThere.road: No direct overland route from India; not applicable

international.permits: Indian passport holders require a UAE Tourist Visa; apply online via the UAE ICP portal (smartservices.icp.gov.ae) or through Emirates, Air Arabia, or a licensed travel agent. The 30-day single-entry visa costs AED 350–550 (~₹7,700–12,100) — the government fee is AED 200 + 5% VAT, but service, insurance, and typing-centre charges push the real total higher; applying directly through ICP is the cheapest route. The 60-day visa costs AED 580–900 (~₹12,700–19,800). Standard processing is 3–4 working days; 48-hour express processing is available. Indians holding a valid visa or residence permit from any of nine qualifying countries — US, UK, any EU/Schengen state, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, Singapore, or South Korea — may instead obtain a Visa on Arrival at UAE airports (AED 100; expanded 13 February 2025); this grants 14 days, extendable once for a further 14 days at AED 250. For the VOA, both the Indian passport and the qualifying third-country document must each be valid for at least 6 months from the UAE entry date, and a physical copy of the third-country document is mandatory at immigration — digital or app-based versions are not accepted at e-gates.

operator.costBand: ₹90k–1.6L pp / 5–6 days all-in (return flights + 4-star hotel + desert safari + transfers; budget travellers in Deira can do ₹65–80k, luxury Palm properties push to ₹2.5L+)
operator.whoToSend: Couples and honeymooners, families with teens, first-time international travellers from Indian metros, luxury shoppers, anyone combining a leisure trip with a Dubai layover extension
operator.pairsWith: Muscat/Oman (5 hr road or 1 hr flight — visa-free for Indians from 2024); Maldives (Emirates hub combo, 4+4 split); Abu Dhabi (2-night extension); Istanbul (Middle East + Europe combo on Emirates/Turkish)

catches: June–September temperatures reach 42°C+ with 90%+ humidity, pushing nearly all activity indoors to air-conditioned malls — warn leisure clients explicitly before booking summer fares.

Ramadan (dates shift each year): eating or drinking in public during daylight hours is a fineable offence, restaurants are closed until sunset, and entertainment is significantly muted — not suited to leisure-first itineraries.

Covered shoulders and knees are mandatory in malls, souks, museums, and on public transport; beachwear is confined to beaches and pools — a common stumbling point for Indian tourists at mall entrances.

Alcohol is sold only at licensed hotel bars and restaurants; there is no off-licence purchase without a personal liquor licence, and public intoxication or possession outside licensed venues can result in arrest and deportation.
December–January hotel rates double or triple versus shoulder months, and New Year's Eve rooms with a Burj Khalifa view sell out months in advance — set client expectations on budget versus timing.

The VOA fee is AED 100 at the counter, not free; more critically, both the Indian passport and the qualifying third-country document (US visa, UK BRP, EU residence card, etc.) must each be valid for 6+ months from the UAE entry date — airlines have denied boarding when either document falls short, and only physical copies are accepted at e-gates.
international.name: Dubai (UAE)

international.region: Bhutan · Western & Central Valleys · 2,200–2,800 m
international.pitch: The world's only carbon-negative kingdom — Tiger's Nest monasteries, butter-lamp dzongs, and prayer-flag passes where Gross National Happiness is government policy, not a slogan.

climate.note: Cold nights, icy high passes, possible Paro valley snowfall; quiet but uncomfortable
Warming slowly; Punakha Tsechu festival window; nights still near freezing
Spring rhododendrons at peak; Paro Tsechu festival; trails excellent
Best overall month: clear skies, wildflowers, Paro Tsechu; book well ahead
Late spring warmth; pre-monsoon clarity; slightly fewer crowds than April
Monsoon onset; trail leeches; landslide risk on Thimphu–Punakha highway
Monsoon peak; daily heavy rain; Tiger's Nest trail slippery; road closures common
Monsoon continues; humidity high; trekking largely impossible; Haa road often cut
Monsoon easing late month; landscapes vivid green; roads reopening; shoulder value
Crisp autumn; Thimphu Tsechu; clearest Himalayan views; peak crowds and prices
Clear and cool; Punakha Dromchoe; black-necked cranes arrive at Phobjikha
Cold and dry; Chelela and Dochula passes may snow-close; very quiet

international.whyGo: Bhutan is the Himalayas without the tourist crush — monasteries perched on 900m cliffs, fortresses built where rivers meet, and a government that by law prioritises ecological preservation over GDP. For Indian travellers it is the easiest international tick: no visa, short flights, familiar vegetarian food, and a profoundly different pace of life.
sights: Tiger's Nest Monastery (Paro Taktsang) — cliffside gompa at 3,120m
Punakha Dzong — 17th-century fortress at the confluence of Mo Chhu and Pho Chhu
Dochula Pass — 108 chortens with panoramic Himalayan skyline on clear days
Buddha Dordenma & National Memorial Chorten, Thimphu
Phobjikha Valley — wintering ground of the endangered black-necked crane
Rinpung Dzong and Ta Dzong (National Museum), Paro
activities: Trek to Tiger's Nest — 4–5h round trip, 900m ascent, cafeteria with cliff views midway
White-water rafting on Mo Chhu or Pho Chhu near Punakha (Grade II–III)
Attend a Tsechu festival — masked Cham dances in dzong courtyards
Traditional hot-stone bath (dotsho) using river-heated stones — standard in most lodges
Archery session — try Bhutan's national sport with a local club
Birding walk in Phobjikha Valley — black-necked cranes Oct–Feb, plus Himalayan Monal

3: Fly into Paro; acclimatize with Rinpung Dzong, Kyichu Lhakhang temple, and Paro town walk; hot stone bath evening
Full-day Tiger's Nest trek (4–5h ascent/descent); cafeteria lunch with cliff views; sunset over Paro valley
Drukgyel Dzong ruins, Ta Dzong museum, local farm lunch; transfer to Paro airport
5: Fly into Paro; Rinpung Dzong, Kyichu Lhakhang, town walk; overnight Paro
Full-day Tiger's Nest trek; evening hot stone bath
Drive to Thimphu (1h): Memorial Chorten, Buddha Dordenma, Tashichho Dzong, handicrafts bazaar
Thimphu → Dochula Pass → Punakha: 108-chorten panorama, Punakha Dzong interior, suspension bridge, Mo Chhu raft
Return via Wangdue to Paro; leisurely valley walk; airport transfer
7: Fly into Paro; Rinpung Dzong, Kyichu Lhakhang, town market walk; overnight Paro
Full-day Tiger's Nest trek (900m ascent); sunset views over Paro valley
Paro valley day: Drukgyel Dzong ruins, Chelela Pass drive (3,988m, Himalayan panorama, rhododendrons); hot stone bath
Drive to Thimphu (1h): National Memorial Chorten, Buddha Dordenma, Folk Heritage Museum, weekend vegetable market
Thimphu → Dochula Pass → Punakha: dzong tour, hanging bridge, white-water raft on Mo Chhu
Drive to Phobjikha Valley (2h): Gangtey Gompa, black-necked crane education centre, marshland nature walk
Return to Paro via Wangdue Phodrang; local farm lunch; depart

international.stay: Paro (airport hub and Tiger's Nest base): backpacker guesthouses from INR 2,000/night up to Uma by COMO and Amankora at INR 35,000–60,000/night. Thimphu: mid-range urban options — Druk Hotel and Taj Tashi at INR 8,000–18,000. Punakha: boutique river lodges (Dhensa Boutique Resort INR 20,000–28,000) for romantic couples. Phobjikha: eco-camps and basic lodges INR 3,000–6,000; book early Oct–Nov for crane season.
dayTrips: Haa Valley (2h from Paro via Chelela Pass, 3,988m) — pristine valley, few tourists; area permit needed, best done as an overnight
Darjeeling, West Bengal — 5h road from Phuentsholing border; natural bookend for a 10–12 day trip
Sikkim (Gangtok / Pelling) — 6h from Phuentsholing via NJP; stack with Bhutan for a full Himalayan circuit
Kaziranga National Park, Assam — 4h from Guwahati border crossing; rhinos + dzongs in one trip
international.pack: Thermal base layer plus mid-layer fleece (mornings drop below 5°C even in April and May), sturdy ankle-support trekking shoes, modest full-coverage clothing for dzong visits (no shorts or sleeveless), high-SPF sunscreen for altitude UV, power bank (load-shedding in off-grid lodges), and a small daypack for the Tiger's Nest ascent.

gettingThere.air: Druk Air and Bhutan Airlines fly Paro from Delhi (2.5h), Kolkata (1h), Mumbai (3h), Bangalore (2.5h); very limited seat inventory — book 3–4 months ahead for March–April and October; fares INR 15,000–35,000 return
gettingThere.rail: Train to New Jalpaiguri (NJP) — overnight (~13h) from Delhi, ~12h from Kolkata; then shared taxi 2.5h to Phuentsholing border town and onward drive to Paro (5h) or Thimphu (4h); total door-to-door ~22h from Delhi
gettingThere.road: Drive from Siliguri/NJP (180km, ~5h) or from Guwahati (180km, ~5h) via Phuentsholing–Jaigaon border crossing; popular itinerary: enter by road, exit by air from Paro; border open daily 0800–1800

international.permits: Indian passport holders need no visa for Bhutan. A valid Indian passport with at least 6 months remaining validity, or an original Voter ID card issued by the Election Commission of India, is accepted at Paro airport and all land border crossings (Phuentsholing, Gelephu, Samdrup Jongkhar, Samtse); Aadhaar cards are not accepted at any entry point. A free Entry Permit is issued on arrival at Paro airport immigration or the relevant land border Immigration Office; online pre-application is available at immi.gov.bt and requires 4–5 working days. The Entry Permit covers Thimphu and Paro districts only — travel to any other district (Punakha, Haa Valley, Bumthang, Phobjikha, eastern districts) requires a separate Route Permit, also free, collected in person at the Department of Immigration office in Thimphu on a working day (Monday–Friday, 9am–5pm; processing takes approximately 1.5–2 hours); a driver or guide may collect it on the client's behalf, but it is issued only at the Thimphu DoI office, not at checkposts. No licensed Bhutanese tour operator is required for Indian nationals. A Sustainable Development Fee (SDF) of INR 1,200 per adult per night (INR 600 for ages 6–12; free under 5) applies and is confirmed fixed until 31 August 2027; a 5% Bhutan GST on hotel stays, transport hire, and guide fees has been in effect since 1 January 2026, replacing the previous 10% accommodation-only sales tax. Reconfirm all rates before quoting, as policy may change.

operator.costBand: ₹45–75k pp / 5–6 days incl. return flights from Delhi or Kolkata, mid-range stays, all permits and transport
operator.whoToSend: Honeymooners and couples seeking a romantic, uncrowded Himalayan escape; spiritual travellers; first-time international travellers wanting a gentle entry; trekkers targeting Tiger's Nest; festival chasers (Tsechu dates shift annually — verify each year)
operator.pairsWith: Darjeeling + Sikkim (10–14 day Northeast Himalayas circuit); Assam Kaziranga (wildlife + culture); Kolkata city break as a gateway add-on

catches: Druk Air and Bhutan Airlines have limited seat inventory and fares spike 2–3x in peak season (March–April, October–November) — lock flights before building any other part of the itinerary.
The SDF of INR 1,200 per adult per night must be itemised as a separate line on every cost sheet; clients who see it for the first time on arrival regularly dispute the invoice.
Monsoon (June–August) landslides routinely close the Thimphu–Punakha highway and the Paro–Haa road for 1–3 days at a time; reroutes add 3–4 hours and can strand a group mid-trip.

The Route Permit for districts outside Thimphu and Paro is issued only at the DoI office in Thimphu on weekdays (Mon–Fri, 9am–5pm) — clients arriving Thursday or Friday for short trips must build in a Thimphu morning for this or risk being turned back at the first checkpost.

Aadhaar is not accepted as a travel document at any Bhutan entry point; brief every client in writing that only a passport (minimum 6 months validity) or an original Election Commission Voter ID card will be accepted.

The 5% Bhutan GST on hotels, transport, and guide fees (effective 1 January 2026) must appear as a separate line on all 2026 quotes — the net impact on total cost varies by package mix compared with the old 10% accommodation-only sales tax.
international.name: Bhutan

international.region: Singapore City-State · Sea Level
international.pitch: Singapore is where you get your first international stamp — spotless, English-speaking, endlessly entertaining, and so easy it feels like cheating.

international.whyGo: A compact, perfectly-engineered city-state that packs world-class theme parks, stunning botanical architecture, and a truly great food culture — Chinese, Malay, Indian, and fusion — into a place where nothing is hard to navigate and nothing feels unsafe. For Indian travellers taking their first overseas trip, it threads the needle: exotic enough to feel like abroad, familiar enough not to overwhelm.

Marina Bay Sands SkyPark (57th-floor observation deck)
Sentosa Island — beaches, Universal Studios, cable car
Chinatown, Little India (Tekka Market), Arab Street (Kampong Glam)
Singapore Botanic Gardens (UNESCO World Heritage Site)

international.stay: Budget (₹3–5k/night): Little India, Geylang, Lavender — guesthouses and budget chains, walkable to MRT. Mid-range (₹6–11k/night): Bugis, Clarke Quay, Chinatown — boutique hotels with MRT access and nightlife proximity. Luxury (₹20–55k+/night): Marina Bay (Marina Bay Sands is the trophy stay), Orchard Road five-stars, and Sentosa resort villas for honeymoon packages.
dayTrips: Johor Bahru, Malaysia — 45-min bus/train across the Causeway; LEGOLAND Malaysia is 1 hr from JB (good for families)
Bintan Island, Indonesia — 1-hr Tanah Merah ferry; beach resort escape with spa and water sports
Batam Island, Indonesia — 45-min ferry; cheap seafood, massage, and shopping (very popular with budget travellers)
Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia — 4.5-hr KTM train or 40-min flight; pairs cleanly as a Singapore + KL combo
international.pack: Light breathable cotton or linen only (humidity 80%+ year-round), compact umbrella (non-negotiable), waterproof sandals, plug adapter Type G (3-pin UK), SPF 50+ sunscreen, one smart-casual outfit for MBS rooftop or upscale dining.

gettingThere.air: Direct flights from Delhi (5.5–6h), Mumbai (5.5h), Chennai (3.5–4h), Bengaluru (4.5h), Hyderabad (5h), Kolkata (3.5–4h) on IndiGo, Air India, Singapore Airlines, Scoot, and Vistara; land at Changi Airport (T1–T4; Jewel connects them all)
gettingThere.rail: No rail link from India; within Singapore, MRT metro covers all tourist hubs — EZ-Link card costs SGD 10 deposit, rides SGD 1–3; Sentosa Express from VivoCity SGD 4
gettingThere.road: Not viable from India; in Singapore, Grab (ride-hailing) fares SGD 8–25 for most city trips; taxis available; Sentosa causeway bus free for pedestrians

international.permits: Indian ordinary passport holders require a Singapore entry visa; there is no visa-free arrangement in place as of 2026. Applications are submitted electronically through an authorized visa agent — the consulate does not accept walk-ins. The non-refundable fee is SGD 30 (~INR 2,100); processing takes 3–5 working days excluding weekends and public holidays, so apply at least 1–2 weeks before departure. Required documents are Form 14A, a valid passport with at least 6 months validity from the date of entry, a recent passport photo, and supporting documents (onward/return ticket, proof of accommodation, proof of funds). Diplomatic, official, and service passport holders are exempt. Indian nationals transiting to a third country may use the 96-hour Visa-Free Transit Facility (VFTF) if they hold a valid visa from Australia, Canada, Germany, Japan, New Zealand, Switzerland, the UK, or the USA. Verify current requirements at ica.gov.sg before confirming any booking.

operator.costBand: ₹85k–1.3 lakh pp / 5-6 days (includes return economy flight from Delhi/Mumbai, mid-range hotel, key attractions, hawker meals)
operator.whoToSend: First-time international travellers, families with children (Universal Studios + Night Safari sell themselves), honeymooners wanting a clean premium experience, and school-holiday groups
operator.pairsWith: Kuala Lumpur + Langkawi (Singapore + Malaysia 8–10 day circuit); Bali (fly Singapore–Denpasar direct); Bangkok or Phuket (Singapore + Thailand back-to-back)

catches: Visa is mandatory for Indian ordinary passport holders — apply through an authorized visa agent via Singapore's e-Visa portal at least 1–2 weeks before departure (processing 3–5 working days, fee SGD 30 / ~INR 2,100 non-refundable); claims of a bilateral visa-free arrangement from August 2024 do not appear in any official source, so always confirm at ica.gov.sg before issuing a confirmation.

S.E.A. Aquarium closed permanently on 30 April 2025 and reopened in July 2025 as Singapore Oceanarium (3× larger, 22 zones, SGD 50–55 pp non-resident depending on peak/off-peak); update every itinerary and quote still referencing S.E.A. Aquarium by name or the old SGD 45 price point.

Attraction costs must be itemised upfront: Universal Studios SGD 83 pp (off-peak) / SGD 86 pp (peak), Night Safari SGD 58 pp non-resident, Singapore Oceanarium SGD 50–55 pp non-resident — pre-book online to lock these rates.

Singapore's zero-tolerance laws catch visitors by surprise: littering fines reach SGD 1,000 for a first offence and SGD 5,000 plus a Corrective Work Order for repeat offences; jaywalking fines can reach SGD 250; chewing gum is banned for sale — brief travellers explicitly before departure.
Afternoon downpours are sudden and heavy even in nominally dry months — include compact umbrellas in welcome kits for every group.
December–January holiday demand spikes hotel rates 40–80% and fills rooms 3–4 months ahead; quote accurately for this window or redirect clients to March.
international.name: Singapore

international.region: Alps & Plateau · 400 m valleys to 4,478 m (Dufourspitze); Zurich sits at 408 m
international.pitch: Where every train window is a postcard — cerulean lakes, cheese-scented valleys, and a snow peak around every bend, all on Europe's most efficient rail network.

international.whyGo: Switzerland distils the best of Europe into a small, obsessively punctual country: glacier-carved valleys, mirror lakes, car-free mountain villages, and cogwheel trains that climb to 3,454 m before breakfast. For Indian travellers it ticks every box — romance, adventure, kids-friendly cable cars, and a food scene that goes well beyond cheese and chocolate.
sights: Jungfraujoch — Top of Europe (3,454 m), Interlaken
Matterhorn & Gornergrat viewpoint, Zermatt
Chapel Bridge (Kapellbrücke) & Lake Lucerne, Lucerne
Jet d'Eau & Palais des Nations, Geneva
Bern Old Town (UNESCO) & Bear Park
Rhine Falls, Schaffhausen — Europe's largest waterfall
activities: Rack-railway to Jungfraujoch (year-round, allow full day)
Tandem paragliding over Interlaken from Beatenberg
Glacier Express or Bernina Express panoramic train
Lake cruise on Lake Lucerne or Lake Geneva by paddlesteamer
Hiking Lauterbrunnen Valley — 72 waterfalls including Staubbach Falls
Swiss chocolate and cheese factory visit (Gruyères or Maison Cailler, Broc)

3: Arrive Zurich → transfer to Lucerne; Chapel Bridge, Lion Monument, lake promenade dinner
Lucerne → Interlaken (2 hrs); afternoon Lake Brienz boat cruise; Harder Kulm sunset gondola

Day trip to Montreux and Château de Chillon; evening back to Geneva
Geneva → Bern old town (UNESCO arcades, Zytglogge clock tower) → Interlaken
Lauterbrunnen Valley waterfalls + Trümmelbach Falls; Grindelwald overnight
Jungfraujoch full day (Top of Europe, Sphinx observation deck, Aletsch Glacier view)
Train to Zermatt via Visp (3.5 hrs); Gornergrat rack railway for Matterhorn sunset
Zermatt → Zurich (3 hrs) via Brig; old town café stop; evening flight departure

international.stay: Lucerne: base for central Alps access; 3-star Seeburg-style lakeside (comfort) or Hotel Des Alpes (budget); Interlaken: gateway to Jungfrau region; Victoria-Jungfrau (luxury), Metropole (comfort), many guesthouses (budget); Zurich: Oerlikon/Altstetten for budget 3-star, Old Town for 4-5 star boutiques; Geneva: Pâquis district mid-range, Rive Gauche for luxury; Zermatt: car-free village, Mont Cervin Palace (luxury), Matterhorn Hostel (budget).
dayTrips: Chamonix, France — 1.5 hrs from Geneva; Mont Blanc cable car, Mer de Glace glacier
Milan, Italy — 2.5 hrs from Lugano or Zurich by train; Duomo, fashion quarter, Last Supper (book ahead)
Annecy, France — 45 min from Geneva; medieval old town, turquoise lake, cycling paths
Stein am Rhein + Rhine Falls — 1 hr from Zurich; medieval frescoed facades + Europe's largest waterfall
international.pack: Waterproof jacket and base layers essential even in July (Jungfraujoch summit sits at 0–5°C year-round); sturdy walking shoes; Swiss Type J power adaptor; high-SPF sunscreen for glacier UV; small day-pack; travel insurance documents printout for visa.

gettingThere.air: Delhi/Mumbai–Zurich (ZRH) direct: Swiss International Air Lines or Air India, ~9 hrs; via Dubai (Emirates) or Doha (Qatar) adds 4–5 hrs with layover; Geneva (GVA) is alternate gateway for French Switzerland itineraries
gettingThere.rail: Swiss Travel Pass (3/7/15-day) covers all trains, buses and lake boats — strongly recommended; Zurich–Interlaken 2 hrs, Zurich–Zermatt 3.5 hrs, Zurich–Geneva 2.5 hrs; Glacier Express Zermatt–St. Moritz 8 hrs (scenic, reservation required)
gettingThere.road: Mandatory CHF 40 motorway vignette sticker (annual); Zurich–Lucerne 50 min, Zurich–Interlaken 2 hrs, Zurich–Geneva 3 hrs; Gotthard tunnel can queue 1–2 hrs in peak summer — time via Gotthard Pass (open May–Oct) if scenic

international.permits: Indian passport holders require a Schengen visa; there is no visa-on-arrival. All applications must be submitted through VFS Global in India — the Swiss Embassy in New Delhi and Consulate in Mumbai do not accept direct walk-ins — so book a VFS appointment slot 6–8 weeks before travel, and earlier still for June–August departures when slots fill quickly. The visa permits a maximum stay of 90 days within any 180-day period (single or multiple entry); mandatory travel/medical insurance of at least €30,000 cover is required at the time of application. Visa fees are €90 for adults and €45 for children aged 6–12. Switzerland has no inner-line or protected-area permits; Jungfraujoch and Gornergrat are accessed by paid rail ticket only, with no separate entry permit. Mountain passes Furka and Grimsel reopen 29 May 2026; Susten reopens 12 June 2026; the Gotthard mountain road (separate from the year-round Gotthard road tunnel) also closes in winter — confirm all pass statuses at alpen-paesse.ch or tcs.ch before finalising itineraries.

operator.costBand: ₹2.2–3.5 lakh pp / 7 days incl. return economy flights (Delhi/Mumbai–Zurich)
operator.whoToSend: Honeymooners and anniversary couples, bucket-list Europe families with teens, senior travellers wanting comfort with no language barrier
operator.pairsWith: Paris (TGV or flight, 3–4 hrs from Geneva), Milan–Venice Italy loop (via Lugano), Austria/Bavaria Germany circuit (Zurich–Innsbruck–Munich)

catches: Switzerland is Europe's most expensive destination: expect CHF 5–6 (approx. ₹500–600) for a coffee and CHF 30–50 (approx. ₹3,000–5,000) for a restaurant meal; budget at least ₹6,000–8,000 per person per day for meals and incidentals beyond the package.

The Jungfraujoch peak-season return ticket from Interlaken is CHF 261.60 per adult (May–Oct 2026); from Grindelwald it is CHF 239.20; with a Swiss Half Fare Card it drops to approximately CHF 130.60 from Interlaken — webshop tickets cancel free until 23:59 the day before, but there is no refund once you board, so check the official summit webcam before committing on a cloudy-forecast day.
Schengen visa refusals and delays are a real risk — do not book non-refundable hotels until the visa is confirmed, and submit the application at least 6–8 weeks before departure.
July–August crowds in Interlaken and Lucerne are intense with large group tours; trains fill fast and hotels at both nodes sell out 3–4 months in advance.

Zermatt is car-free: the standard Täsch-to-Zermatt transfer is the Matterhorn Gotthard shuttle train (12 minutes, every 20 minutes, CHF 17.20 return; luggage rolls on free trolleys with a CHF 5 deposit) — electric taxis operate only within Zermatt village once you arrive, not as the Täsch transfer; for very heavy luggage a pre-booked road taxi from Täsch directly to your Zermatt hotel costs CHF 20–40 extra.

Susten Pass stays closed until mid-June, so a classic Grimsel–Susten loop in late May or early June is not possible even though Furka and Grimsel are already open by then — confirm current status at alpen-paesse.ch before booking.
international.name: Switzerland

international.region: Southern Europe · sea level–4,800 m (Alps/Dolomites)
international.pitch: Where every piazza is a stage, every meal a memory, and 3,000 years of art lies within walking distance — Italy is the trip your clients will still be talking about a decade later.

climate.note: Cold, overcast, post-Christmas lull; some rain in the centre
Still cool; Venice Carnival draws crowds and colour mid-month
Spring arrives; Easter can fall here — expect heavy Rome crowds
Wildflowers, mild sun, manageable crowds — textbook spring travel
Best weather before summer surge; long golden evenings
Warm and increasingly busy; southern Italy heat builds fast
Peak heat and maximum crowds; school holiday price surge
Ferragosto closures, oppressive heat, overpriced everywhere
Grape harvest, golden light, crowds ease sharply after mid-month
Autumn colour, truffle season, comfortable walking temperatures
Rainy and grey; off-peak prices, quiet museums, Venice acqua alta risk
Christmas markets in Rome and Florence; cold but festive and photogenic

international.whyGo: Italy delivers an unmatched density of art, history, and food in a single country — Roman ruins, Renaissance masterpieces, Baroque churches, and Michelin-starred trattorias within a two-hour train ride of each other. Indian travellers connect with the family-centric culture, the generous table, and the ease of stacking it onto a wider Europe leg.
sights: Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine Hill, Rome
Vatican Museums, Sistine Chapel & St Peter's Basilica, Rome
Uffizi Gallery & Ponte Vecchio, Florence
Venice Grand Canal, St Mark's Basilica & Doge's Palace
Amalfi Coast: Positano–Ravello coastal drive
Cinque Terre five cliff-villages, Liguria
activities: Pre-booked guided Vatican tour with early-access Sistine Chapel entry
Gondola through Venice's narrow back canals (rio) at dusk
Chianti wine tasting at a Tuscan estate with vineyard lunch
Colosseum underground or arena-floor access tour
Coastal trail hike between Vernazza and Monterosso, Cinque Terre
Hands-on pasta or gelato cooking class in Florence or Bologna

3: Day 1: Rome — Colosseum, Roman Forum, Palatine Hill; aperitivo and dinner in Trastevere
